original_hypothesis.title: PairFactory setStakingFees Has No Combined Fee Cap Allowing LP Fee Diversion
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: setStakingFees allows stakingNFTFee up to 3000 (30%). Combined with MAX_REFERRAL_FEE (unbounded per INV-ACCESS-001), the combined deduction from LP fees can exceed 100% of the swap fee amount. In _update0/_update1: referralFee is deducted first, then stakingNFTFee is computed on the remainder. If both are set high (e.g., referral=7000, staking=3000), LPs receive only amount * 0.3 * 0.7 = 21% of the original fee. While each setter has its own cap, no combined cap prevents near-total LP fee diversion to referral and staking systems.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Fee distribution in _update0/_update1 applies referral and staking deductions sequentially with no floor on LP portion. FeeManager can legally set parameters that leave LPs with near-zero yield, discouraging liquidity provision.
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: PairFactory.sol: setStakingFees requires _newFee <= 3000; setReferralFee has no upper bound

proof_of_signal.pattern: require(_newFee <= 3000) but no combined cap with MAX_REFERRAL_FEE; setReferralFee has no upper bound check

preconditions: FeeManager sets both referralFee and stakingNFTFee to high values

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: referralFee = amount * MAX_REFERRAL_FEE / 10000 (unbounded); stakingFee = (amount - referral) * stakingNFTFee / 10000 (up to 30%); LP gets remainder
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Set MAX_REFERRAL_FEE=9700, stakingNFTFee=3000 → LP receives ~2.1% of fee amount
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Divert >97% of swap fees away from LPs to referral and staking systems controlled by feeManager

economic_questions: What is the current MAX_REFERRAL_FEE setting?
Who controls the feeManager role and is it timelocked?
What is the maximum combined diversion possible?
validation_requirements: Calculate LP share for various fee parameter combinations
Verify underflow behavior when referralFee > amount
Check if any on-chain safeguards limit combined fee extraction

preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Up to 100% of all swap fees across all pairs
preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_profit_margin: Extremely high if fees are redirected
preliminary_attack_economics.attack_duration: Immediate effect on all future swaps

market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: LPs exit due to zero yield; protocol TVL collapses

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: All LP holders across all pairs
potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: Near-total loss of expected swap fee yield
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: All accumulated and future swap fees

investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Compute LP share under various combined fee scenarios

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
